page: Amazon Web Services m5dn.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8259CL processors operating at 3.1 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, utilizing dedicated CPU allocation with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided locally via 3600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the network configuration delivers a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core performance and memory throughput. The server achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 81985.6 and a PassMark CPU score of 56892.01, placing both in the top 25 percent. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 4542.21 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 95.59 GB/sec. Database and web serving capabilities are also strong, demonstrated by 77.66 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a static web serving throughput of 48.44 GB/sec. Single-core performance and text processing benchmarks fall within the average range.

While multi-threaded compute and database operations are highly performant, the server exhibits limitations in specific workloads. Large language model (LLM) text generation speeds are poor, yielding only 33.79 tokens per second on small models and 5.07 tokens per second on medium models, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. Qualitatively, the bare-metal configuration offers high resource density and direct hardware access, making it cost-efficient for workloads that require high network throughput, local NVMe storage, and intensive multi-core processing. It is suited for database hosting, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and memory-intensive general-purpose applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.metal is a bare-metal general-purpose server featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz. It includes 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 56892.01, and high-speed memory bandwidth. Database operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a Redis SET performance of 77.66 million operations per second. However, the server exhibits poor performance in LLM text generation due to the absence of GPU acceleration. This instance is suited for high-throughput database hosting, static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded general-purpose workloads requiring direct hardware access.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, 3600 GB NVMe SSD, and 100 Gbps bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, 3600 GB NVMe storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A bare-metal server combining high-speed local NVMe storage, 100 Gbps networking, and strong multi-core processing for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8259CL processors at 3.1 GHz.
384.0 GB DDR4 memory and 3600 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput applications.
Strong multi-core CPU and database performance, including 77.66 million Redis SET operations per second.
Poorly suited for LLM text generation due to the lack of GPU acceleration.
Suited for database hosting, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
